'''Carl''' was a [[Shikitari (species)|Shikitari]] [[Insectoid/Legends|insectoid]] who was purchased to participate as a [[Gladiator/Legends|gladiator]] in free-for-all mortal brawls in [[the Cauldron]] arena, the largest combat pit on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Rattatak/Legends|Rattatak]], shortly after the outbreak of the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]]. However, Carl was quickly killed by a [[Bugnaught]] [[Droid/Legends|droid]], which burned him alive.

==Biography==
This [[Shikitari (species)|Shikitari]] [[Insectoid/Legends|insectoid]] was born on a [[Shikitari|jungle world]] in the [[Unknown Regions/Legends|Unknown Regions]] and lived<ref name="DB" /> during the final years of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Old Republic]].<ref name="NEC">''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''</ref> He belonged to a rare [[Sentience/Legends|subsentient]] species, notable for its multi-stage life cycle.<ref name="DB" />

At one point, a [[Devaronian conman]] learned of their specific physiology and decided to carry out a scam involving a Shikitari insectoid. One of them, who was given the unlikely name Carl,<ref name="DB" /> was a collection of [[Pupa/Legends|pupae]] that had amalgamated into a [[Humanoid/Legends|humanoid]] form<ref name="CE" /> and was perfectly suited for [[Gladiator/Legends|gladiatorial]] combat.<ref name="DB" /> Since the Shikitari stage as a single lifeform was extremely short,<ref name="CE" /><ref name="DB" /> the Devaronian took advantage of the widespread ignorance surrounding their unique physiology and sold Carl for a sizable sum, snickering to himself that in two [[Standard day/Legends|days]] his buyer would be the proud owner of a hardened chrysalis from which twelve harmless creatures would emerge.<ref name="DB" />

[[File:CarlDemise.jpg|280px|left|thumb|A Bugnaught droid incinerated Carl]]
After being purchased, Carl was immediately entered into the [[Rattatak/Legends|Rattataki]] gladiatorial games, and in this way he ended up in [[The Cauldron]].<ref name="CE" /><ref name="DB" /> It was the largest gladiatorial pit on Rattatak, filled with violent warriors, where war barons and generals sought out the best combatants who would win their wars. But it was a rare gladiator indeed who could survive multiple fights there.<ref name="DBR">{{DB|location|rattatak|Rattatak}}</ref> Therefore, shortly after the outbreak of the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]],<ref name="NEC" /> Carl, like many other gladiators, entered a deadly free-for-all battle, facing off in particular against the combat droid [[L8-L9]].<ref name="EGD">''[[The New Essential Guide to Droids]]''</ref> However, an encounter with a [[Bugnaught]] [[Droid/Legends|droid]] proved decisive for the Shikitari. Carl lifted the apparently defenseless machine over his head, roaring furiously. It then proceeded to unleash its [[Flamethrower/Legends|flamethrower]] arm, killing Carl.<ref name="CW" />

==Personality==
The alien, which looked like a hulking insectoid,<ref name="EGD" /> was in fact a collection of pupae that had amalgamated into a humanoid form capable of combat.<ref name="CE" /> That single organism, known as Carl, was perfectly suited for gladiatorial combat but remained dim-witted,<ref name="DB" /> unable to plan his moves in battle, and therefore relied on his brute strength. In battle, his body was covered only by a simple [[Color/Legends|brown]] loincloth.<ref name="CW" />

==Behind the scenes==
Carl<ref name="CE" /> first appeared as an unnamed background character in the [[Chapter 6 (Star Wars: Clone Wars)|sixth chapter]]<ref name="CW" /> of ''[[Star Wars: Clone Wars]]'', an animated television micro-series<ref name="Chapter Archive">{{SWArchive|url=clonewars/microseries/chapter/archive.html|text=View ''Clone Wars'' Chapter Archive}}</ref> produced by [[Cartoon Network]] Studios<ref name="Volume One">[[Star Wars: Clone Wars Volume One|''Star Wars: Clone Wars'' Volume One]]</ref> and originally aired on [[November 14]], [[2003]].<ref name="TV">{{TVcom|url=shows/star-wars-clone-wars/chapter-vi-276612|text=Star Wars: Clone Wars - Season 1, Episode 6: Chapter VI}}</ref> The character was later given a name and short backstory in ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'', a [[2008]] reference book written by [[Stephen J. Sansweet]], [[Pablo Hidalgo]], [[Bob Vitas]], and [[Daniel Wallace]].<ref name="Penguin">{{PenguinRandomHouse|url=books/160596/the-complete-star-wars-encyclopedia-by-stephen-j-sansweet-pablo-hidalgo-bob-vitas-daniel-wallace/9780345477637|text=The Complete Star Wars® Encyclopedia}}</ref>
